name: Heart Failure - Medication-Only Patients (No Diagnosis)
description: Tricky test case: finds patients on HF guideline-directed medical therapy who have NO formal heart failure diagnosis on their problem list (Path C). Real-world cardiology often has many GDMT-treated patients without an explicit HF diagnosis — meds may have been started for hypertension (carvedilol, ACE-I), arrhythmia (beta blockers), edema (furosemide), or empirically. An LLM that queries only Condition resources for HF will miss this entire population.
prompt: I want to find patients in our system who are taking heart failure medications such as sacubitril/valsartan, carvedilol, or empagliflozin but don't have a formal heart failure diagnosis on their problem list. These are often patients managed empirically or whose HF was never coded.

prompts.naive: Find patients on heart failure medications without a heart failure diagnosis.
prompts.expert: I need to identify a documentation gap that is common with HF GDMT: patients on ARNi, MRA, SGLT2 inhibitors, or HF-dose carvedilol who lack a formal HF Condition resource. Two-step approach: first find all patients with MedicationRequest for HF medications (RxNorm 4603 furosemide, 20352 carvedilol, 9997 spironolactone, 1656339 sacubitril/valsartan, 1545653 empagliflozin), then exclude those with a Condition for HF subtypes (84114007, 42343007, 88805009, 703272007, 446221000).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Path C 'tricky' patients: have HF medications but NO HF Condition resource. The Synthea module weights this path moderately (~30%) because some HF patients (especially those started on diuretics for edema or carvedilol for hypertension) end up on de facto GDMT without a coded HF diagnosis.
